name: Okayama Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: dignity, history and the noble traditions of the prefecture

description: A deep-purple field charged at the centre with a yellow emblem combining the katakana オ and カ of the prefecture's name.
history: Okayama's prefectural symbol was adopted on 21 September 1967. The flag, with the yellow emblem on a purple field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: a deep purple chosen as a dignified background for the prefectural emblem

features.role: Okayama prefectural emblem (stylised katakana オカ)
features.symbolism: a stylised arrangement of the katakana オ (o) and カ (ka) of 岡山 (Okayama), forming a balanced circular design that suggests harmony, growth and the development of the prefecture.
